C-XGBoost: Boosted causal outcome model with propensity scoring

Source: R/causalXGBoost.R
CXGBoost.Rd

C-XGBoost: Boosted causal outcome model with propensity scoring

C-XGBoost: Boosted causal outcome model with propensity scoring

Value

Object returned by function_name.

Details

R6 class implementing a causal inference model that uses xgboost for the outcome model (two-head: Y(0) and Y(1)) and ranger for the propensity score. A DragonNet-style treatment mask ensures each unit's gradient only updates the head corresponding to its observed treatment.

Model architecture

  • Outcome model: XGBoost with num_target = 2 and a custom masked-MSE objective. Head 0 is updated for control units (\(T=0\)); head 1 for treated units (\(T=1\)).

  • Propensity model: Ranger probability forest predicting \(P(T=1 \mid X)\).

See also

RCausalML-package

Public fields

parameters

List of XGBoost hyperparameters merged with defaults.

booster

Fitted xgb.Booster (post-fit).

propensity_model

Fitted ranger model (post-fit).

scale_pos_weight

Reserved for future use.

imputation_table

Per-column medians for NA imputation (post-fit).

train_colnames

Column names of training X (post-fit).

oob_error

OOB classification error of the propensity model.

fit_time_secs

Training wall-clock time in seconds.

Methods

Public methods

  • CXGBoost$new()

  • CXGBoost$fit()

  • CXGBoost$predict()

  • CXGBoost$summary()

  • CXGBoost$evaluate()

  • CXGBoost$plot_importance()

  • CXGBoost$save_model()

  • CXGBoost$clone_reset()

  • CXGBoost$clone()

Method new()

Create a new CXGBoost model.

Usage

CXGBoost$new(
  parameters = list(),
  propensity_model = NULL,
  scale_pos_weight = NULL
)

Arguments

parameters

Named list of XGBoost hyperparameters. Merged with defaults (eta=0.05, max_depth=6, subsample=0.8, colsample_bytree=0.8, min_child_weight=1, lambda=1, alpha=0, tree_method="hist").

propensity_model

NULL to fit internally, or a pre-fitted ranger probability forest.

scale_pos_weight

Reserved; currently unused.

Method fit()

Fit the C-XGBoost outcome model and propensity model.

Usage

CXGBoost$fit(X, t, y, nrounds = 100L, verbose = 0L, eval_data = NULL)

Arguments

X

Numeric covariate matrix (n x p). NAs are median-imputed.

t

Binary treatment vector (0/1), length n.

y

Observed outcome vector (numeric), length n.

nrounds

XGBoost boosting rounds (default 100).

verbose

Integer verbosity: 0 = silent, 1 = progress (default 0).

eval_data

Optional named list list(X=..., t=..., y=...) for XGBoost watchlist early-stopping diagnostics (no early stopping is applied evaluation loss is printed when verbose >= 1).

Returns

self invisibly (supports method chaining).

Method predict()

Predict potential outcomes and propensity scores.

Usage

CXGBoost$predict(X)

Arguments

X

Numeric covariate matrix (n x p). NAs are imputed using the table built during fit(). Column order must match training X.

Returns

A named list with components:

y0_hat

Predicted Y(0) potential outcome under control.

y1_hat

Predicted Y(1) potential outcome under treatment.

propensity_score

P(T=1|X), clipped to (1e-6, 1-1e-6).

tau_hat

Individual treatment effect Y(1) - Y(0).

Method summary()

Print a formatted model summary.

Usage

CXGBoost$summary()

Returns

self invisibly.

Method evaluate()

Evaluate model against ground-truth potential outcomes.

Usage

CXGBoost$evaluate(y_true, X)

Arguments

y_true

n x 2 matrix [Y(0), Y(1)] of true potential outcomes, or a numeric vector of true ITEs (tau_true).

X

Covariate matrix for prediction. If NULL, the method cannot predict supply X from the training set or a test set.

Returns

Named list: PEHE, ATE_error, tau_hat, y0_hat, y1_hat.

Method plot_importance()

Plot variable importance from the XGBoost outcome model.

Requires ggplot2.

Usage

CXGBoost$plot_importance(top_n = 20L, metric = "Gain")

Arguments

top_n

Number of top variables to display (default 20).

metric

Importance metric: "Gain", "Cover", or "Frequency" (default "Gain").

Returns

A ggplot object (invisibly).

Method save_model()

Save the fitted model to an RDS file.

Usage

CXGBoost$save_model(path)

Arguments

path

File path (e.g. "cxgboost_model.rds").

Returns

self invisibly.

Method clone_reset()

Return an unfitted copy with the same hyperparameters.

Useful for cross-validation loops where the same configuration is reused across folds without retaining fitted state.

Usage

CXGBoost$clone_reset()

Returns

A new unfitted CXGBoost object.

Method clone()

The objects of this class are cloneable with this method.

Usage

CXGBoost$clone(deep = FALSE)

Arguments

deep

Whether to make a deep clone.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# function_name(...)
} # }
